Workflow.title: Workflow
Workflow.description: Represents an autonomous enterprise workflow instance, tracking its execution, status, and audit chain.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Workflow entity.

type.description: The predefined type of the workflow, indicating its template.

status.description: The current execution status of the workflow.

priority.description: The priority level of the workflow.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the workflow was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the workflow was last updated.

startedAt.description: Timestamp when the workflow execution officially started, or null if not yet started.

completedAt.description: Timestamp when the workflow execution was completed, or null if not yet completed.

slaDeadline.description: The calculated Service Level Agreement (SLA) deadline for workflow completion.

slaStatus.description: The current SLA status of the workflow.

slaBreachPredictedAt.description: Timestamp when an SLA breach is predicted to occur, or null if no breach is predicted.

triggeredBy.description: Identifier of the user or system that triggered the workflow (e.g., userId, 'system', 'webhook').

currentStepIndex.description: The zero-based index of the currently active step in the workflow sequence.

currentAgentId.description: Reference to the Agent.id currently processing a step in this workflow. (Relationship: Agent 1:N Workflow)

totalSteps.description: The total number of steps defined for this workflow.

completedSteps.description: The number of steps that have been successfully completed in the workflow.

input.description: A JSON string representing the initial input data for the workflow.

output.description: A JSON string representing the cumulative output data generated by the workflow steps.

errorCount.description: The total number of errors encountered during the workflow execution.

selfHealingAttempts.description: The number of times the Healer agent was invoked for this workflow.

lastError.description: A JSON string containing details of the last error encountered ({ message, stepId, timestamp, agentId }), or null.

humanInterventions.description: The number of times a human had to intervene in this workflow's execution.

auditChainHead.description: The cryptographic hash of the latest AuditEntry, ensuring chain integrity.

tags.description: A list of tags associated with the workflow for categorization.

assignedTo.description: Reference to the UserProfile.id responsible for oversight of this workflow. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Workflow)

metadata.description: A JSON string containing arbitrary key-value metadata for extensions.

WorkflowStep.title: WorkflowStep
WorkflowStep.description: Represents an individual step within a workflow, detailing its execution and outcome.

id.description: Unique identifier for the WorkflowStep entity, typically its index within the workflow.

workflowId.description: Reference to the Workflow.id this step belongs to. (Relationship: Workflow 1:N WorkflowStep)

index.description: The zero-based sequential index of this step within its workflow.

name.description: A short, descriptive name for the workflow step (e.g., 'Validate Purchase Request').

description.description: A detailed description of what this step is intended to accomplish.

agentType.description: The type of agent primarily responsible for executing this step.

status.description: The current execution status of this specific step.

startedAt.description: Timestamp when this step began execution, or null.

completedAt.description: Timestamp when this step completed execution, or null.

durationMs.description: The duration of the step's execution in milliseconds, or null if not completed.

agentReasoning.description: The agent's chain-of-thought or reasoning process for decisions made during this step.

agentConfidence.description: A confidence score (0-1) provided by the agent for its actions or decisions in this step.

input.description: A JSON string representing the input data provided to this step.

output.description: A JSON string representing the output data produced by this step.

errorMessage.description: An error message if the step failed, or null.

retryCount.description: The number of times this step has been retried.

maxRetries.description: The maximum number of retries allowed for this step before escalating.

isHumanRequired.description: Indicates if this step requires human approval or intervention.

humanApprovalStatus.description: The status of human approval if required for this step, or null if not applicable.

humanApproverId.description: Reference to the UserProfile.id of the human approver, or null. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N WorkflowStep)

humanApprovalNote.description: Notes provided by the human approver, or null.

humanApprovalAt.description: Timestamp when human approval was given, or null.

toolsUsed.description: A list of Genkit tool names invoked by the agent during this step.

tokensUsed.description: The number of LLM tokens consumed during the execution of this step.

AuditEntry.title: AuditEntry
AuditEntry.description: An immutable, cryptographically chained record of every decision and action taken by agents or humans within a workflow.

id.description: Unique identifier for the AuditEntry entity.

workflowId.description: Reference to the Workflow.id this audit entry belongs to. (Relationship: Workflow 1:N AuditEntry)

sequence.description: A monotonically increasing sequence number for audit entries within a workflow.

timestamp.description: Timestamp when this audit entry was created.

agentId.description: Reference to the Agent.id that performed the action or decision. (Relationship: Agent 1:N AuditEntry)

agentType.description: The type of the agent that performed the action (e.g., 'orchestrator', 'executor').

action.description: A description of the action performed by the agent or human.

decision.description: A summary or chain-of-thought explaining why the action was taken.

stepId.description: Reference to the WorkflowStep.id associated with this audit entry. (Relationship: WorkflowStep 1:N AuditEntry)

data.description: A JSON string representing a snapshot of relevant data at the time of the action.

previousHash.description: The cryptographic hash of the preceding audit entry in the chain, or 'GENESIS' for the first entry.

currentHash.description: The cryptographic SHA-256 hash calculated for this audit entry.

isHumanAction.description: Indicates if this audit entry was generated by a human action.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile.id if the action was performed by a human. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N AuditEntry)

ipAddress.description: The IP address from which a human action originated, if applicable.

immutable.description: A flag indicating that this entry is immutable (always true).

Agent.title: Agent
Agent.description: Represents an individual AI agent within the SENTINEL system, tracking its status and performance.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Agent entity (e.g., 'orchestrator-1').

type.description: The functional type of the agent (e.g., 'orchestrator', 'perceiver').

status.description: The current operational status of the agent.

currentWorkflowId.description: Reference to the Workflow.id the agent is currently working on, or null. (Relationship: Workflow 1:1 Agent)

currentStepId.description: Reference to the WorkflowStep.id the agent is currently processing, or null. (Relationship: WorkflowStep 1:1 Agent)

totalTasksCompleted.description: The total number of tasks successfully completed by this agent.

totalTasksFailed.description: The total number of tasks that failed when processed by this agent.

successRate.description: The success rate of the agent, represented as a number between 0 and 1.

averageTaskDurationMs.description: The average duration in milliseconds for tasks completed by this agent.

lastActiveAt.description: Timestamp of the agent's last recorded activity.

capabilities.description: A list of Genkit tool names that this agent is capable of using.

healthScore.description: A calculated health score (0-100) for the agent, reflecting its performance and operational state.

lastHeartbeat.description: Timestamp of the agent's last reported heartbeat, indicating it is online.

AgentActivityLog.title: AgentActivityLog
AgentActivityLog.description: A detailed log of individual actions performed by an AI agent.

id.description: Unique identifier for the AgentActivityLog entity.

agentId.description: Reference to the Agent.id that performed this activity. (Relationship: Agent 1:N AgentActivityLog)

action.description: Description of the action performed by the agent.

workflowId.description: Reference to the Workflow.id related to this activity, or null if not workflow-specific. (Relationship: Workflow 1:N AgentActivityLog)

stepId.description: Reference to the WorkflowStep.id related to this activity, or null if not step-specific. (Relationship: WorkflowStep 1:N AgentActivityLog)

outcome.description: The outcome of the agent's action (success, failure, or partial).

durationMs.description: The duration of the agent's action in milliseconds.

errorMessage.description: An error message if the action resulted in a failure, or null.

tokensUsed.description: The number of LLM tokens consumed for this activity.

SLAConfig.title: SLAConfig
SLAConfig.description: Defines Service Level Agreement (SLA) configurations for different workflow types.

id.description: Unique identifier for the SLAConfig entity, typically the workflow type name.

workflowType.description: The type of workflow to which this SLA configuration applies.

totalSlaHours.description: The total number of hours allowed for the entire workflow to complete.

stepSlaMinutes.description: A JSON string mapping step names to their allowed completion time in minutes (e.g., { 'Step Name': 15 }).

atRiskThresholdPercent.description: The percentage of total SLA time elapsed at which a workflow is considered 'at risk'.

escalationUserId.description: Reference to the UserProfile.id to be alerted on SLA breach for this workflow type. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N SLAConfig)

autoEscalateEnabled.description: Indicates if automatic escalation is enabled for SLA breaches of this workflow type.

Notification.title: Notification
Notification.description: Represents an in-app or external notification generated by the SENTINEL system.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Notification entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile.id who is the recipient of this notification. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Notification)

type.description: The category or type of the notification.

title.description: The title or subject of the notification.

message.description: The main content or body of the notification message.

workflowId.description: Reference to the Workflow.id related to this notification, or null. (Relationship: Workflow 1:N Notification)

stepId.description: Reference to the WorkflowStep.id related to this notification, or null. (Relationship: WorkflowStep 1:N Notification)

read.description: Indicates if the notification has been read by the recipient.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the notification was created.

expiresAt.description: Timestamp when the notification is set to expire, or null if it does not expire.

UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Stores application-specific profile information for a user, linked to Firebase Authentication.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserProfile entity, matching the Firebase Auth UID.

role.description: The user's role within the application, determining access levels.

department.description: The department the user belongs to.

notificationPreferences.description: A JSON string indicating the user's preferences for different notification channels (e.g., { email: true, inApp: false, slack: true }).

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the user profile was created.

lastLoginAt.description: Timestamp of the user's last login.

Integration.title: Integration
Integration.description: Stores configuration details for external system integrations.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Integration entity, typically the integration name (e.g., 'gmail', 'slack').

name.description: The human-readable name of the integration.

enabled.description: Indicates if the integration is currently active.

config.description: A JSON string containing the integration's configuration parameters. This data should be encrypted or store references to secret manager.

lastSyncAt.description: Timestamp of the last successful synchronization with the external system, or null.

status.description: The current connection status of the integration.

EpisodicMemory.title: EpisodicMemory
EpisodicMemory.description: Stores summarized past workflow runs and healing attempts, used by agents for learning and decision-making.

id.description: Unique identifier for the EpisodicMemory entry, typically the workflowId it summarizes.

workflowType.description: The type of workflow associated with this memory.

workflowId.description: Reference to the Workflow.id that this memory is about. (Relationship: Workflow 1:N EpisodicMemory)

summary.description: A one-paragraph summary of what happened during the workflow run.

outcome.description: The overall outcome of the workflow run (success, failure, or partial).

durationMs.description: The total duration of the workflow run in milliseconds.

errorEncountered.description: Indicates if any errors were encountered during the workflow run.

healingStrategiesUsed.description: A list of healing strategies that were employed during this workflow run.

humanInterventions.description: The number of human interventions that occurred during this workflow run.

lessonsLearned.description: A one-paragraph summary of lessons learned from this workflow run for future reference.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when this episodic memory entry was created.

ApprovalRequest.title: ApprovalRequest
ApprovalRequest.description: Represents a request for human approval within a workflow.

id.description: Unique identifier for the ApprovalRequest entity.

workflowId.description: Reference to the Workflow.id that this approval request is part of. (Relationship: Workflow 1:N ApprovalRequest)

stepId.description: Reference to the WorkflowStep.id that triggered this approval request. (Relationship: WorkflowStep 1:N ApprovalRequest)

approverId.description: Reference to the UserProfile.id of the individual required to approve. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N ApprovalRequest)

title.description: A brief title for the approval request.

details.description: Detailed explanation or context for the approval request.

urgency.description: The urgency level of the approval request.

deadline.description: Timestamp by which the approval is required.

status.description: The current status of the approval request.

requesterId.description: Reference to the UserProfile.id of the user or agent who initiated this approval request. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N ApprovalRequest)

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the approval request was created.

resolvedAt.description: Timestamp when the approval request was resolved (approved, rejected, or timed out), or null.

approverNote.description: Notes provided by the approver when resolving the request, or null.

definition.description: Stores application-specific profile information for a user. Document ID is the Firebase Auth UID. Includes denormalized 'uid' field for consistent data access.
Represents an autonomous enterprise workflow instance. Includes 'assignedTo' and 'triggeredBy' for potential authorization rules and 'auditChainHead' for cryptographic chain integrity.

params.name: workflowId
params.description: The unique identifier for the workflow.

definition.description: Represents an individual step within a workflow. Document ID is the step's index. Includes 'humanApproverId' for direct human approval authorization.

params.name: workflowId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent workflow.

params.name: stepId
params.description: The unique identifier (index) for the workflow step.

definition.description: An immutable, cryptographically chained record of actions within a workflow. Includes 'workflowId', 'agentId', and 'userId' (if human) for self-contained authorization.

params.name: workflowId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent workflow.

params.name: auditEntryId
params.description: The unique identifier for the audit entry (sequential).

definition.description: Represents an individual AI agent within the SENTINEL system. Document ID is the agent's unique ID.
A detailed log of individual actions performed by an AI agent. Includes 'agentId' for self-contained authorization.

params.name: agentId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent agent.

params.name: logId
params.description: The unique identifier for the agent activity log entry.

definition.description: Defines Service Level Agreement configurations for different workflow types. Document ID is the workflow type itself.
Represents an in-app or external notification. Includes denormalized 'userId' field for recipient-specific authorization independence.

params.name: notificationId
params.description: The unique identifier for the notification.

definition.description: Stores configuration details for external system integrations. Document ID is the integration name.

params.name: integrationId
params.description: The unique identifier (name) for the integration.

definition.description: Stores summarized past workflow runs and healing attempts, partitioned by workflow type for efficient agent learning. Includes 'workflowType' (in path) and 'workflowId' for comprehensive context.

params.name: workflowType
params.description: The type of workflow to which this episodic memory applies.

params.name: memoryId
params.description: The unique identifier for the episodic memory entry.

definition.description: Represents a request for human approval within a workflow. Includes denormalized 'approverId' and 'requesterId' for authorization independence.

params.name: approvalRequestId
params.description: The unique identifier for the approval request.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore data structure is designed to adhere to the core principles of Authorization Independence, Structural Segregation, and Access Modeling, prioritizing security, scalability, and debuggability for the SENTINEL platform. It leverages nested subcollections for hierarchical data and top-level collections with denormalized authorization fields where global or cross-cutting access patterns are needed.

**Authorization Independence (CRITICAL):**

1.  **Users (`/users/{userId}`):** User profiles are stored in a top-level collection where the document ID directly matches the Firebase `request.auth.uid`. This provides immediate path-based ownership, making authorization rules like `request.auth.uid == userId` atomic and independent of `get()` calls.
2.  **Notifications (`/notifications/{notificationId}`):** Each notification document explicitly contains a `userId` field, representing the recipient. Security rules can directly check `resource.data.userId == request.auth.uid` without needing to fetch any parent user data, ensuring authorization independence for personalized alerts.
3.  **Approval Requests (`/approvalRequests/{approvalRequestId}`):** Similar to notifications, `ApprovalRequest` documents include `approverId` and `requesterId` fields. This allows security rules to grant access based on these fields (e.g., `resource.data.approverId == request.auth.uid`) directly from the document, avoiding `get()` calls to workflows or users.
4.  **Workflows and Steps (`/workflows/{workflowId}`, `/workflows/{workflowId}/steps/{stepId}`):** For the `Workflow` entity, fields like `assignedTo` and `triggeredBy` are present. If read access were restricted to, for example, `resource.data.assignedTo == request.auth.uid`, it would be authorization independent. Currently, the rules are broad (`isAuthenticated()`), so `get()` is not needed for the existing rules, but the *structure* supports granular, independent authorization if rules are tightened. For `WorkflowStep`, the `humanApproverId` field enables direct, independent authorization for human intervention steps. The `workflowId` is part of the path, implicitly linking it to its parent.
5.  **Audit Entries (`/workflows/{workflowId}/auditEntries/{auditEntryId}`):** Each `AuditEntry` document includes `workflowId` and `userId` (if human action). This makes it self-contained for security rules, as all relevant authorization context is within the document itself or its direct path.

**QAPs (Query Across Partitions) and Structural Segregation:**

1.  **Structural Segregation:** Data is segregated into distinct collections based on its security posture and logical grouping:
    *   `users`: Stores user-specific profiles, managed individually.
    *   `workflows`: Contains high-level workflow instance data. This is a primary entry point for querying all active workflows.
    *   `agents`: Dedicated collection for agent status and metadata.
    *   `slaConfig`: Centralized configuration for SLAs, allowing easy lookup by `workflowType` (used as document ID).
    *   `integrations`: Admin-only configuration for external systems.
    *   `notifications` and `approvalRequests`: Top-level collections allow for global queries (e.g., by admins) while still enabling efficient user-specific filtering via the `userId` or `approverId`/`requesterId` fields.

2.  **Hierarchical Paths for User-Owned/Nested Data:**
    *   **Workflow Details:** `workflows/{workflowId}/steps` and `workflows/{workflowId}/auditEntries` are subcollections. This structure allows efficient querying of all steps or audit logs *for a specific workflow* using collection-level queries scoped to the parent document. It naturally enforces the 1:N relationship and simplifies data retrieval for the workflow detail views.
    *   **Agent Activity Logs:** `agents/{agentId}/activityLog` provides a dedicated, nested log for each agent, allowing efficient querying of an agent's historical activities.
    *   **Episodic Memory (`/episodicMemoryByWorkflowType/{workflowType}/memories/{memoryId}`):** This is a critical structural segregation for QAPs. By creating a top-level collection `episodicMemoryByWorkflowType` and then subcollections `/{workflowType}/memories`, agents can efficiently query memories *specific to a given workflow type* (`retrieveEpisodicMemory(workflowType)`). This partitions the memory data by its most common access pattern, significantly improving query performance and scalability for AI agent learning processes.
